question: Rashid Shaheed: Anytime Touchdown
description:
In the upcoming NFL game, scheduled for January 25 at 6:30 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Yes" if Rashid Shaheed scores a touchdown at any point during regulation or overtime.

This market will resolve to "No" if Rashid Shaheed does not score a touchdown during regulation or overtime.

Qualifying Touchdowns:

- A rushing touchdown by the player

- A receiving touchdown caught by the player

- A special teams touchdown (e.g. punt/kick return)

Passing touchdowns do not qualify. Defensive touchdowns will be credited to the defensive unit, not individually credited to the player who scores.

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.

If the player is listed as inactive or otherwise does not take a snap, the market will resolve "No".

The resolution source will be the official NFL game book as published on NFL.com.

question: Series: Inner Circle Kill Handicap (-26.5) vs VP.Prodigy (+26.5)
description:
This market refers to the Dota 2 match between Inner Circle and VP.Prodigy in the FISSURE Universe Episode 8 Play-In Group A, scheduled for January 21 at 4:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Inner Circle" if Inner Circle secures 27 or more kills than VP.Prodigy in the match. 

Otherwise, it will resolve to "VP.Prodigy".

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50.

question: International T20 Series: South Africa vs West Indies (Game 1)
description:
This market refers to the cricket match between South Africa and West Indies scheduled for January 27 2026 in International T20 Series.

This market resolves according to the finalized match result as published by https://www.icc-cricket.com/.  

DLS/DRS, over-rate penalties, forfeit/walkover, or any other on-field ruling that leads the competition to declare a winner are treated as ordinary wins.

If the match ends tied and the playing conditions provide an on-field tiebreak (e.g., Super Over), the winner determined by that tiebreak will be used for resolution. If the match ends tied and no on-field tiebreak is used or available under the playing conditions (e.g., group-stage ODI with no Super Over), the market will resolve 50-50.

If the match is postponed/rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed. If the match is permanently canceled or abandoned or otherwise is completed without a winner, the market resolves 50-50

question: International T20 Series: South Africa vs West Indies (Game 1) - Most Sixes South Africa Winner
description:
This market refers to which team hits the greater number of sixes in the cricket match between South Africa and West Indies scheduled for 2026-01-27 in South Africa T20 Series.
This market resolves according to the finalized match statistics as published by https://www.icc-cricket.com/.
The outcome corresponding to South Africa will be considered correct if South Africa is officially recorded as hitting more sixes than West Indies.The outcome corresponding to West Indies will be considered correct if West Indies is officially recorded as hitting more sixes than South Africa.
If both teams record the same number of sixes, the market will resolve to "Draw".
The market will resolve according to the team that hits the greater number of sixes in the match regardless of if the match is not competed (e.g. Due to weather conditions)/
Only sixes scored from the bat (off any delivery- legal or not) will count towards the total sixes. Overthrows and extras do not count. Sixes scored in a super over do not count.
In First Class games, only first innings sixes will count.
If the match is permanently canceled, abandoned, or otherwise completed without official sixes statistics being recorded, the market will resolve "Draw".
If the match is postponed or rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed and official statistics are available.

question: SA20: Pretoria Capitals vs Sunrisers Eastern Cape (Game Qualifier 1)
description:
This market refers to the cricket match between Pretoria Capitals and Sunrisers Eastern Cape scheduled for January 21 2026 in SA20.

This market resolves according to the finalized match result as published by https://www.espncricinfo.com/.  

DLS/DRS, over-rate penalties, forfeit/walkover, or any other on-field ruling that leads the competition to declare a winner are treated as ordinary wins.

If the match ends tied and the playing conditions provide an on-field tiebreak (e.g., Super Over), the winner determined by that tiebreak will be used for resolution. If the match ends tied and no on-field tiebreak is used or available under the playing conditions (e.g., group-stage ODI with no Super Over), the market will resolve 50-50.

If the match is postponed/rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed. If the match is permanently canceled or abandoned or otherwise is completed without a winner, the market resolves 50-50

question: SA20: Pretoria Capitals vs Sunrisers Eastern Cape (Game Qualifier 1) - Most Sixes Pretoria Capitals Winner
description:

The outcome corresponding to Pretoria Capitals will be considered correct if Pretoria Capitals is officially recorded as hitting more sixes than Sunrisers Eastern Cape.The outcome corresponding to Sunrisers Eastern Cape will be considered correct if Sunrisers Eastern Cape is officially recorded as hitting more sixes than Pretoria Capitals.

In First Class games, only first innings sixes will count.
If the match is permanently canceled, abandoned, or otherwise completed without official sixes statistics being recorded, the market will resolve "Draw".
If the match is postponed or rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed and official statistics are available.

question: Series: LNG Esports Kill Handicap (-12.5) vs LGD Gaming (+12.5)
description:
This market refers to the LoL match between LNG Esports and LGD Gaming in the LPL Group Nirvana, scheduled for January 21 at 4:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "LNG Esports" if LNG Esports secures 13 or more kills than LGD Gaming in the match. 

Otherwise, it will resolve to "LGD Gaming".

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50.

The resolution source for this market is information from Chainlink, specifically the XRP/USD data stream available at https://data.chain.link/streams/xrp-usd.
Please note that this market is about the price according to Chainlink data stream XRP/USD, not according to other sources or spot markets.

question: Spread: AC Milan (-1.5)
description:
In the upcoming Serie A game, scheduled for February 3 at 2:45 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "AC Milan" if AC Milan win the game by 2 or more goals.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Bologna FC 1909".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

This market will resolve according to the official final score published on legaseriea.it. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

question: Svitolina vs. Shnaider: Total Sets O/U 2.5
description:
This market refers to the tennis match between Elina Svitolina and Diana Shnaider in the Australian Open WTA, scheduled for January 23 2026.
This market will resolve to "Over" if the total number of sets completed equals or exceeds 3. Otherwise, it will resolve to "Under."
A super tie breaker is considered as one (1) set for total set markets.
If the match begins but is not completed, this market will resolve 50-50. If the match is canceled before play begins or delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a result, this market will also resolve 50-50.
Resolution will be based on official Grand Slam statistics.
groupItemTitle: Australian Open Women's: Elina Svitolina vs Diana Shnaider Total Sets: O/U 2.5

events.title: Australian Open Women's: Elina Svitolina vs Diana Shnaider
events.description:
This market refers on the tennis match between Elina Svitolina and Diana Shnaider in the Australian Open WTA, scheduled for January 22 at 7:00PM ET.

This market will resolve to 'Svitolina' if Elina Svitolina advances against Diana Shnaider.

This market will resolve to 'Shnaider' if Diana Shnaider advances against Elina Svitolina.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If the match begins but is not completed, and one player advances due to the opponent's retirement, default, or disqualification, this market will resolve to the player who advances.

If the match ends in a walkover (player withdraws before the start and the other advances automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the Australian Open. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

question: Set Handicap: Svitolina (-1.5) vs Shnaider (+1.5)
description:
This market refers to the tennis match between Elina Svitolina and Diana Shnaider in the Australian Open WTA, scheduled for January 23 2026.
This market will resolve to "Svitolina" if Elina Svitolina wins by 2 or more sets than Diana Shnaider, based on the final completed score. Otherwise, it will resolve to "Shnaider."
If the match begins but is not completed, this market will resolve 50-50. If the match is canceled before play begins or delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a result, this market will also resolve 50-50.
Resolution will be based on official Grand Slam results.

events.title: Australian Open Women's: Elina Svitolina vs Diana Shnaider
events.description:
This market refers on the tennis match between Elina Svitolina and Diana Shnaider in the Australian Open WTA, scheduled for January 22 at 7:00PM ET.

This market will resolve to 'Svitolina' if Elina Svitolina advances against Diana Shnaider.

This market will resolve to 'Shnaider' if Diana Shnaider advances against Elina Svitolina.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If the match begins but is not completed, and one player advances due to the opponent's retirement, default, or disqualification, this market will resolve to the player who advances.

If the match ends in a walkover (player withdraws before the start and the other advances automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the Australian Open. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

This market will resolve to "Putintseva" if Yulia Putintseva wins by 2 or more sets than Zeynep Sonmez, based on the final completed score. Otherwise, it will resolve to "Sonmez."
If the match begins but is not completed, this market will resolve 50-50. If the match is canceled before play begins or delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a result, this market will also resolve 50-50.
Resolution will be based on official Grand Slam results.

events.title: Australian Open Women's: Zeynep Sonmez vs Yulia Putintseva
events.description:
This market refers on the tennis match between Zeynep Sonmez and Yulia Putintseva in the Australian Open WTA, scheduled for January 22 at 7:00PM ET.

This market will resolve to 'Sonmez' if Zeynep Sonmez advances against Yulia Putintseva.

This market will resolve to 'Putintseva' if Yulia Putintseva advances against Zeynep Sonmez.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If the match begins but is not completed, and one player advances due to the opponent's retirement, default, or disqualification, this market will resolve to the player who advances.

If the match ends in a walkover (player withdraws before the start and the other advances automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the Australian Open. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

question: XRP Up or Down - January 22, 2:00AM-2:15AM ET
description:
This market will resolve to "Up" if the XRP price at the end of the time range specified in the title is greater than or equal to the price at the beginning of that range. Otherwise, it will resolve to "Down".
The resolution source for this market is information from Chainlink, specifically the XRP/USD data stream available at https://data.chain.link/streams/xrp-usd.
Please note that this market is about the price according to Chainlink data stream XRP/USD, not according to other sources or spot markets.
